commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=951bb29e20d753de174e184e634efe47e197c378)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the embedded agent message-update handler to stream ordinary non-phase assistant text through chunk-local stripping while preserving full-buffer recompute for split tag, reasoning-close, and fenced-code edge cases, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +51, Tests +140. Total +191 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the narrow source-level issue: current main strips block tags from the accumulated deltaBuffer on each non-phase text update, and the branch adds tests proving chunk-local streaming. I did not run the broader Windows/local-model event-loop repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainer handling is appropriate because the PR is draft/protected and the remaining action is validation and landing judgment, not an automated repair.

target.title: fix(agents): stream assistant deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary

- stream ordinary non-phase assistant text deltas incrementally instead of stripping the accumulated `deltaBuffer` on every chunk
- keep full recompute behavior for split block-tag fragments, reasoning-close replacements, and fenced-code literals where chunk-local stripping is unsafe
- add regressions for suffix streaming, split orphan close replacement, media-only replacement, and backtick/tilde fenced code

Fixes part of https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.filters-final-suppresses-output-without-start-tag.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.emits-block-replies-text-end-does-not.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.keeps-assistanttexts-final-answer-block-replies-are.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- AWS Crabbox `check:changed`: run `run_1bc72a909baa`, lease `cbx_f4ffdc173209`, slug `pearl-crayfish-d441`; blocked by unrelated current-main core typecheck errors in `src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.ts` and `src/plugins/discovery.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: repeated subscriber work over accumulated streamed text during normal non-phase assistant deltas.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest/static checks plus AWS Crabbox remote Linux changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: commands listed in Verification.

Evidence after fix: focused tests pass with 10 files / 206 tests; autoreview reports no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: ordinary text deltas append from the new chunk, while split tag/fenced-code cases still use full recompute.

What was not tested: full `pnpm check:changed` could not complete because current `origin/main` typecheck fails in files outside this branch.
target.author: vincentkoc
